english: in general mǰúg-la (mjug la), mǰúg-tu (mjug tu) adv. and postp., = mťar (mthar), at the end of, at last, behind, after, with the genit. inf., or the verbal root, gen. opp. to mgo (mgo). — mǰúg-sgro (mjug sgro) (W. *júg-ro* (*jug ro*)) lower or inferior part, underpart, buttocks (cf. gžug (gzhug)); mǰúg-to (mjug to) id. — mǰug-btág (mjug btag) (for btég (bteg)), and mǰug-ldéb (mjug ldeb) W. wagtail. — mgo-mǰúg (mgo mjug) above and below Dzl.

resp. gsáṅ-mǰe (gsang mje), the penis; Zam. avoids the term by making use of circumlocutions, others employ it, esp. Med.; also in vulgar use; mǰe láṅ-ba (mje lang ba) erection of the penis; mǰe sbúbs-su nub (mje sbubs su nub) the penis recedes; mǰe-mgo (mje mgo) glans penis. — mǰe-rlíg (mje rlig) the penis and testicles. — mǰe - śubs (mje shubs) the membraneous covering or sheath of the penis.

Zam. suffering, enduring, bearing patiently; Cs.: ob-noxious; mi-mǰéd (mi mjed) prop.: free; gen. the world, the universe, acc. to Buddhistic ideas; except in the last mentioned sense the word seems to be little used.

pf. ǰags (jags), Cs.; Sch.: to establish, settle, fix, found; hence prob. bde-ǰágs (bde jags) and kris-ǰágs (khris jags), ǰags-kris (jags khris) (Lexx. and elsewh., but not frq.) time of prosperity, of peace, of rest, a time without disturbances, war, epidemics etc. (kris (khris) by itself is not known).
1. Lex. = klu (klu), or n. of a Lu, also ǰóg-po (jog po). — 2. vulgo = yág-po (yag po).

1. Sch.: a sort of coarse and thick grass of inferior quality; so Pth. of a hut: ǰág-mas p̌úb-pa (jag mas phub pa) covered with such grass. — 2. Lex. a fragrant grass, Andropogon muricatus. — 3. Glr.: a blade (of grass), stalk (of corn), ǰág-ma reï stéṅ-na (jag ma re'i steng na) on every blade, kú-śaï ǰág-ma p̌oṅ čig (ku sha'i jag ma phong cig) a bundle of blades of Kusha grass; ǰag-rgód (jag rgod) Sch. horse-tail, pewter-grass, Equisetum. — 4. Sik. squirrel, perh. = bya-ma-byi (bya ma byi) Sch. (?).
C. to give, to make a present Georgi Alph. Tib.

pf. prob. bžabs (bzhabs), fut. bžab (bzhab), to sneak, slink, creep privily; to lie in wait, in ambush, tsé-la ǰáb-pa (tse la 'jab pa) to attempt a person's life Pth.; *pág-ne ǰáb-te sad táṅ-če* (*pag ne 'jab te sad tang ce*) W. to assassinate; lkog ǰab byéd-pa (lkog 'jab byed pa) v. lkog (lkog); ǰáb-bus ma byín-par lén-pa ('jab bus ma byin par len pa) Thgy. to steal clandestinely. Cog. to °čáb-pa ('chab pa).

B., *ǰám-po* (*'jam po*) W., *ǰam-ǰám* (*'jam 'jam*) C. col. (opp. to rtsúb-pa (rtsub pa), rtsíṅ-ge (rtsing ge)) soft, smooth, tender, mild, e.g. of cloth, hair, a meadow, a plain without stones or rocks, of fruit, the air, the character of a person, a person's way of speaking (ṅag (ngag) C., *pé-ra* (*pe ra*) W., *pé-ra ǰám-po daṅ* (*pe ra 'jam po dang*) with mild expressions, fair words, in a friendly manner), of a law; of beverages: weak W.; of a (hay-)rake: close W.; *ǰám-po ṅáb-če* (*'jam po ngab ce*) W. to mow off close; ǰam-búd ('jam bud) blowing or playing (the flute) softly, piano; ǰam-rtsí ('jam rtsi) Med., seems to be a kind of medicine; *ǰam-sáṅ* (*'jam sang*) W., C., plain, without ornaments.

ǰam-mgón ('jam mgon), ǰam(-pai)-dbyáṅs ('jam pa'i dbyangs) one of the two great Bodhisattvas of the northern Buddhists, the Apollo of the Tibetans, the god of wisdom, demiurge, and more particularly the tutelar god and civilizer of Nepal (v. Köpp. II, 21), incarnated in Thonmi Sambhota, and afterwards in king Kri-sroṅ-sde-btsán (khri srong sde btsan) and others. Cf. spyan-ras-gzigs (spyan ras gzigs).
